# Gasclaw

> Single-container deployment: Gastown + OpenClaw + KimiGas

Gasclaw runs a Gastown multi-agent workspace managed by an OpenClaw overseer bot on Telegram. All agents use Kimi K2.5 as their LLM backend through Claude Code's agentic interface.

## Links

- Repo: https://github.com/gastown-publish/gasclaw
- Gastown: https://github.com/steveyegge/gastown
- Gastown Docs: https://docs.gt.villamarket.ai
- OpenClaw: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw
- Kimi CLI: https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-cli
- Kimi Docs: https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/
- AIS: https://github.com/gastown-publish/ais
- KimiGas: https://github.com/gastown-publish/kimigas
- Beads: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads
- Gastown Monitor: https://github.com/gastown-publish/gastown-monitor
- N-Skills: https://github.com/gastown-publish/n-skills
- Wiki: https://github.com/gastown-publish/gasclaw/wiki

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────── Docker Container ────────────────┐
│  OpenClaw Gateway (port 18789) ← Telegram        │
│  Gastown HQ — Mayor + Crew (Claude Code / Kimi)  │
│  Dolt SQL (port 3307) — agent state via beads    │
│  KimiGas — LRU key rotation with cooldown        │
│  /project ← volume mount (your repo)             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Components:
- AIS (ais CLI): PRIMARY EXECUTION LAYER — all agents run in tmux via ais
- Gastown (gt CLI): Multi-agent workspace — Mayor, Crew, Daemon, Deacon, Witness, Refinery
- OpenClaw: Overseer — Telegram bot, monitoring, compliance, skill system
- KimiGas: LRU key rotation with 5-min rate-limit cooldown
- Dolt: Version-controlled SQL for agent state
- Beads (bd CLI): Git-backed issue tracking

## Environment Variables

Required:
- GASTOWN_KIMI_KEYS: Colon-separated Kimi API keys for Gastown agents (sk-xxx:sk-yyy)
- OPENCLAW_KIMI_KEY: Kimi API key for OpenClaw overseer (separate pool, never shared)
- TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN: Telegram bot token from @BotFather
- TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID: Numeric Telegram user ID

Optional:
- GT_RIG_URL: Git URL or path for rig (default: /project)
- GT_AGENT_COUNT: Number of crew workers (default: 6)
- MONITOR_INTERVAL: Health check interval in seconds (default: 300)
- ACTIVITY_DEADLINE: Max seconds between commits (default: 3600)
- DOLT_PORT: Dolt SQL server port (default: 3307)
- LOG_LEVEL: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR (default: INFO)

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone git@github.com:gastown-publish/gasclaw.git && cd gasclaw
cp .env.example .env   # Add your keys
docker compose up -d
```

Development:
```bash
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
make dev && make test   # 1021 unit tests, no API keys needed
```

## Project Structure

```
src/gasclaw/
├── cli.py           # Typer CLI: start, stop, status, update
├── config.py        # Env var config (dataclass, no pydantic)
├── bootstrap.py     # 10-step startup orchestration
├── health.py        # Health checks + activity compliance
├── gastown/
│   ├── agent_config.py  # Write settings/config.json for gt
│   ├── installer.py     # Kimi account setup + gt install
│   └── lifecycle.py     # Start/stop dolt, daemon, mayor
├── kimigas/
│   ├── key_pool.py      # LRU key rotation with rate-limit cooldown
│   └── proxy.py         # ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL env builder + Claude config
├── openclaw/
│   ├── installer.py     # Write openclaw.json + Telegram config
│   ├── skill_manager.py # Copy skills to ~/.openclaw/skills/
│   └── doctor.py        # Config verification
└── updater/
    ├── checker.py       # Check versions of gt, claude, openclaw, dolt
    ├── applier.py       # Run update commands
    └── notifier.py      # POST to OpenClaw gateway / Telegram
reference/               # Distilled dependency docs for agents
scripts/                 # Validation scripts
skills/                  # OpenClaw skills (health, keys, update, agents, ais-orchestrator)
tests/unit/              # 1021 unit tests (all mocked)
```

## Bootstrap Sequence (10 steps)

1. Setup Kimi proxy — init KeyPool, set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, write Claude config
2. Install Gastown — gt install + gt rig add (cwd must be gt root)
3. Configure agent — gt config agent set kimi-claude claude
4. Start Dolt — SQL server on configured port
5. Configure OpenClaw — write ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
6. Install skills — copy to ~/.openclaw/skills/
7. Run doctor — openclaw doctor --repair
8. Start daemon — gt daemon start
9. Start mayor — gt mayor start --agent kimi-claude
10. Notify — send "Gasclaw is up" via Telegram

Rollback on failure: previously started services are stopped automatically.

## Kimi Proxy

Claude Code reads these env vars:
- ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://api.kimi.com/coding/
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=<kimi-key>
- CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=~/.claude-kimigas
- DISABLE_COST_WARNINGS=true

Permission bypass via ~/.claude-kimigas/.claude.json:
- bypassPermissionsModeAccepted: true
- customApiKeyResponses.approved: [last-20-chars-of-key]
- hasCompletedOnboarding: true

Do NOT use --dangerously-skip-permissions (rejected under root).

Key rotation: LRU with 5-min cooldown. BLAKE2b hash tracking. Separate pools for agents vs overseer.

## OpenClaw Telegram Config

Critical fields in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json:
- channels.telegram.dmPolicy: "open" (requires allowFrom: ["*"])
- channels.telegram.groupPolicy: "open" (NOT "owner" — invalid, silently ignored)
- channels.telegram.groups.*.requireMention: false (default is true — bot won't reply without this)
- messages.ackReactionScope: "all" (controls emoji only, NOT replies)
- channels.telegram.streaming: "off" (recommended for Telegram)

Common mistakes:
- ackReactionScope controls reactions, NOT whether bot replies
- requireMention defaults to true — must explicitly set false for group auto-reply
- allowFrom is for DM user IDs only, never group chat IDs
- groupPolicy only accepts: open, disabled, allowlist
- entrypoint.sh overwrites openclaw.json on container restart

## Gastown CLI Reference

Install: go install github.com/steveyegge/gastown/cmd/gt@latest (NOT pip install gastown)
Beads: go install github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd@latest

Key commands:
- gt install — init workspace
- gt rig add <url> — register project (must run from gt root dir)
- gt config agent set <name> <cmd> — register agent
- gt agents — list running agents (NOT gt status --agents)
- gt daemon start/stop/status — daemon lifecycle
- gt mayor start --agent <name> — start mayor

Dolt: pkill -f "dolt sql-server" (not dolt sql-server --stop — unreliable)

## Health Checks

| Service | Method | Healthy |
|---------|--------|---------|
| Dolt | dolt sql -q "SELECT 1" | Query succeeds |
| Daemon | gt daemon status | Running |
| Mayor | gt mayor status | Running |
| OpenClaw | GET localhost:18789/health | HTTP 200 |
| Activity | git log --since=<deadline> | Commits exist |

## Development

- TDD: write test first, then implementation
- All subprocess/httpx calls mocked in unit tests
- No API keys needed for unit tests
- Never modify a test to make it pass — fix the code
- Branch naming: fix/, feat/, test/, docs/, refactor/
- Commit format: <type>: <description>

## AIS — Primary Execution Layer (tmux)

Source: https://github.com/gastown-publish/ais

EVERYTHING runs in tmux via ais. Never run agents outside ais.

Key commands:
- ais create <name> -a claude|kimi -A <account> [--yolo] [-c "cmd"] [-d dir]
- ais ls — list all sessions
- ais inspect <name> -n 100 — capture last 100 lines
- ais inspect <name> --rate-limit — check for rate limits
- ais inject <name> "text" — send command to session
- ais watch <name> -i 5 — live tail every 5s
- ais kill <name> — graceful shutdown
- ais kill --all — kill everything
- ais accounts — list configured accounts
- ais status <name> — machine-readable JSON status
- ais logs <name> -o file.log — save full scrollback

Account setup:
- Claude: ~/.claude-accounts/<id>/ (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR)
- Kimi: ~/.kimi-accounts/<id>/ (KIMI_SHARE_DIR)

Log trail at ~/.ais/logs/<name>/: events.log, snapshots.log, status.json, prompt.txt

Prompt rules for sub-agents:
1. One task, one agent
2. Be specific about what (name the files)
3. Set boundaries (which dirs to touch)
4. Define done ("Print TASK COMPLETE: <summary>")
5. Give context (paste errors, don't say "run tests to see")
6. Match agent to task (Opus=complex, Sonnet=fixes, Kimi=free boilerplate)

Rate limit recovery: kill session, respawn with different account.
Rotation: Claude cc1→cc2→cc3→cc1, Kimi 1→2→3→1.

## Validation

```bash
make test                              # 1021 unit tests
make lint                              # Ruff linting
bash scripts/validate-openclaw-config.sh  # OpenClaw config validation
openclaw doctor --repair               # Health check + fix
openclaw channels status --probe       # Telegram probe
```

## Config Change Rules

1. Read official docs FIRST — never guess config values
2. Validate after every change — run validation script
3. Test end-to-end — send a real message, check logs
4. Never mix concerns — allowFrom is DM only, groups config is separate
5. Check logs — openclaw logs or tail gateway log
6. Invalid values are silently ignored — only testing reveals problems
